Submit an approved internal AI-assisted request through the centralized AI Gateway. This internal platform API is required because the MID_LEVEL_DESIGN states that domain services call the AI Gateway over internal HTTPS REST only and defines AI Gateway internal request and response contracts, but does not expose a public API route for raw AI invocation. The API is used by approved domain services such as Pre-assessment Service, Credit Interpretation Service, Recommendation Service, Auditor Q and A Service, and Standards and CIR Q and A Service. The AI Gateway applies tenant isolation, policy enforcement, prompt sanitization, PII minimization, approved GPT 5.1 routing, response validation, citation validation, explainability logging, AIInteractionLog persistence, and controlled degradation handling.

useCases.description: Authenticate with local account credentials to access the platform.
Create, list, and update local user accounts and assignments.
Configure roles, permissions, and scoped access grants with auditability.
Create, list, and update projects and project metadata.
Enter project master data including rating system, regional profile, and related setup data.
Maintain supported rating systems, versions, and addenda.
Create and maintain region-specific profiles, climate mappings, and weather references.
Run AI-assisted pre-assessment using project master data and optional uploaded artifacts to generate feasibility outputs, scores, graphs, and action items.
Execute credit interpretation based on project context, rules, addenda, and preferences with rationale and sources.
Create, list, assign, and update evidence collection tasks with reminders, escalations, and validation rules.
Upload project artifacts such as PDFs, CAD or BIM files, spreadsheets, images, and emails for processing and evidence use, including source metadata and optional import-template-governed validation.
Search evidence artifacts by project, credit, tags, and metadata.
Review, approve, reject, or correct OCR and structured extraction results.
Review, approve, reject, or correct artifact-to-credit classification results.
View dynamic project scorecard and trigger recalculation when needed.
Request a manual score override with justification, attachment, reason code, and approval workflow.
Review prioritized compliance pathways and alternatives with rationale, tradeoffs, expected points impacts, and backing simulations or calculators where applicable.
Run and save what-if scenario comparisons for target credits or parameter changes.
Submit simulation jobs, track execution status, and review generated outputs.
Generate narratives, calculators, reports, scorecards, checklists, simulation summaries, and form-ready outputs in supported formats.
Submit generated documents for human review and record approve or reject decisions.
Assemble a submission package with naming rules, versioning, checksums, and immutable history for manual portal upload.
Request and complete dual approval for a submission package before finalization.

Configure future-ready certification portal placeholders, template-set mappings, and enablement controls while keeping manual packaging mode at MVP.
Request restoration of soft-deleted records within the tenant-configured restore window subject to policy controls.

Approved domain services submit AI-assisted requests to the internal AI Gateway so that all AI processing is centralized, policy-controlled, tenant-isolated, auditable, explainable, and routed only through the approved GPT 5.1 gateway path. This use case supports AI-enabled business flows such as pre-assessment, credit interpretation, recommendations, auditor response suggestions, and standards Q and A without exposing raw AI provider calls as public APIs.
